titel: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on university admissions: selection criteria, distribution of places, and implementation.
html:
In the letter, the government outlines its assessment of the observations and recommendations made by the Swedish National Audit Office (Riksrevisionen) in the report "Selection for Higher Education – Selection Criteria, Distribution of Places, and Implementation" (RiR 2025:10). The Swedish National Audit Office's overall conclusion is that the current selection system generally functions well, but that it could be more efficient in certain areas. The Swedish National Audit Office recommends that the government ensure that any changes to the selection regulations are implemented based on comprehensive impact assessments, that an appropriate authority is tasked with regularly evaluating the selection regulations, and that data on the selection group and merit score of university students is preserved for research and statistical purposes.

The government welcomes the review and largely agrees with the Swedish National Audit Office's observations and recommendations. Regarding the recommendation on impact assessments, the government emphasizes that great importance is attached to investigating potential consequences for the overall system when parts of it are changed, and that this will continue to be the case.

With this letter, the government considers the Swedish National Audit Office's report to be finalized.
tldr: The government has reviewed a report from the Swedish National Audit Office regarding how students are admitted to higher education institutions. Most of the current regulations work well, but certain aspects need improvement to make the system even better.
lattlast:
The Swedish National Audit Office is an agency that reviews how the government operates. They investigated how students are selected for various programs at the university and concluded that the current system works well in most cases, but that there are areas that could be made more efficient.

To improve the system, the National Audit Office proposes that the government always examines the overall impact of any changes before altering the rules. They also want a government agency to be tasked with regularly monitoring how the rules function in practice. Furthermore, it is important to retain information about students' backgrounds and grades so that it can be used for research and statistics.

The government agrees with most of the proposals from the audit. They emphasize that they will always consider how changes affect the entire system when making decisions. With this announcement, the work on this particular report is concluded.
